attacks.name: Sunsteel Strike
attacks.effect: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sol Burst-GX
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 5 Energy cards and attach them to your Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Moongeist Beam
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't be healed during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Lunar Fall-GX
attacks.effect: Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Basic Pokémon that isn't a Pokémon-GX. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Your opponent reveals their hand. You may have your opponent count the cards in their hand, shuffle those cards into their deck, then draw that many cards.

You may play as many Item cards as you like during your turn (before your attack).

You may play 4 Missing Clover cards at once.
• If you played 1 card, look at the top card of your deck.
• If you played 4 cards, take a Prize card. (This effect works one time for 4 cards.)

attacks.effect: Move any number of Energy from your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.effect: Shuffle all Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.effect: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Timeless-GX
attacks.effect: Take another turn after this one. (Skip the between-turns step.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

attacks.name: Rocket Fall
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

attacks.name: Blaster-GX
attacks.effect: Turn all of your Prize cards face up. (Those Prize cards remain face up for the rest of the game.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Dark Flash
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Moon's Eclipse-GX
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Rumbling Wires
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Lighting-GX
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. Add a card you find there to their Prize cards face down. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Frost Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Polar Spear-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Fast Raid
attacks.effect: If you go first, you can use this attack on your first turn.

attacks.name: Cruel Spike
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

attacks.name: Beauty-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each Prize card your opponent has taken. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Grand Bloom-GX
attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Basic Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Outrage
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dragon Pulse
attacks.effect: Discard the top 2 cards of your deck.

flavorText: This Pokémon is friendly to people and loves children most of all. It comes from deep in the mountains to play with children it likes in town.

attacks.name: Turbo Drive
attacks.effect: Attach a basic Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

attacks.name: Rebel-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Merciless Strike
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 30 more damage.

flavorText: The heavy control mask it wears suppresses its intrinsic capabilities. This Pokémon has some hidden special power.

attacks.name: Resource Management
attacks.effect: Put 3 cards from your discard pile on the bottom of your deck in any order.

attacks.name: Profound Knowledge
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Deep in the jungle, high in the lofty canopy, this Pokémon abides. On rare occasions, it shows up at the beach to match wits with Slowking.

attacks.name: Identify
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand. If you find a Pokémon there, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: It adores having Rattata and Raticate for dinner, but as it's diurnal, it never encounters them. This Pokémon boasts incredible patience.

attacks.name: Scout
attacks.effect: Your opponent reveals their hand.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: It wanders around in a never-ending search for food. At dusk, it collapses from exhaustion and falls asleep on the spot.

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

attacks.name: Spinning Fan
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

attacks.name: Own the Place
attacks.effect: If your opponent has a Stadium card in play, discard it. If you do, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Toss Aside
attacks.effect: Discard random cards from your opponent's hand until they have 3 cards in their hand.

flavorText: To make itself appear intimidatingly beefy, it tightly cinches its waist with its twin tails.

attacks.name: Gentle Bite
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon's attacks do 40 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

flavorText: When it's happy, Glameow demonstrates beautiful movements of its tail, like a dancing ribbon.

attacks.name: Stompy Stomp
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.

attacks.name: Happy Turn
attacks.effect: You may shuffle this Pokémon and all cards attached to it into your deck.

flavorText: The ears appear to be delicate. If they are touched roughly, it kicks with its graceful legs.

attacks.name: Weak Kneed
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: When it senses danger, it perks up its ears. On cold nights, it sleeps with its head tucked into its fur.

attacks.name: Palette of Friends
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 damage for each different type of Pokémon on your Bench.

flavorText: Possessing an unbalanced and unstable genetic makeup, it conceals many possible evolutions.
Current studies show it can evolve into an incredible eight different species of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Dangerous Lick
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 50 more damage for each heads. If the first flip is tails, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: Their saliva contains lots of components that can dissolve anything. The numbness caused by their lick does not dissipate.

attacks.name: Lap Up
attacks.effect: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Slam
attacks.effect: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 50 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its tongue is twice the length of its body. It can be moved like an arm for grabbing food and attacking.

attacks.name: Spatial Control
attacks.effect: Move any number of Energy from your Benched Pokémon to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Hydro Pressure
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Water Energy attached to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Zero Vanish-GX
attacks.effect: Shuffle all Energy from each of your opponent's Pokémon into their deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overclock
attacks.effect: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

attacks.name: Shred
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by any effects on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Timeless-GX
attacks.effect: Take another turn after this one. (Skip the between-turns step.) (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Quick Dive
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Royal Blades
attacks.effect: If you played Cynthia from your hand during this turn, this attack does 100 more damage.

flavorText: The protuberances on its head serve as sensors. It can even detect distant prey.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: Shiny objects are its passion. It can be found in its cave, scarcely moving, its gaze fixed on the jewels it's amassed or Carbink it has caught.
It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It skulks in caves, and when prey or an enemy passes by, it leaps out and chomps them. The force of its attack sometimes chips its teeth.

attacks.name: Exeggutor's Paradise
attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Exeggcute, search your deck for an Alolan Exeggutor or Alolan Exeggutor-GX and put it onto that Exeggcute to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Draco Meteor Barrage
attacks.effect: Flip a coin for each Grass Energy attached to this Pokémon. This attack does 80 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Alola is the best environment for this Pokémon. Local people take pride in its appearance, saying this is how Exeggutor ought to look.

attacks.name: Psywave
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Magical Swap
attacks.effect: Move any number of damage counters on your opponent's Pokémon to their other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: This guardian deity of Akala is guilelessly cruel. The fragrant aroma of flowers is the source of its energy.

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Forests where Shiinotic live are treacherous to enter at night. People confused by its strange lights can never find their way home again.

attacks.name: Flickering Spores
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It scatters spores that flicker and glow. Anyone seeing these lights falls into a deep slumber.
This artificial Pokémon, constructed more than 500 years ago, can understand human speech but cannot itself speak.

attacks.name: Meteor Tempest
attacks.effect: Discard 3 Energy from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Sun's Eclipse-GX
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Radiant Star
attacks.effect: For each of your opponent's Pokémon in play, attach a Metal Energy card from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Corona Impact
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
flavorText: This Pokémon is said to be a male evolution of Cosmog. At the activation of its third eye, it departs for another world.

attacks.name: Guard Press
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Boiling Impact
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Boiling blood, like magma, circulates through its body. It makes its dwelling place in volcanic caves.

attacks.name: Psy Bolt
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Psychic Resonance
attacks.effect: If your opponent has any Psychic Pokémon in play, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Ancient people believed that petitioning Bronzong for rain was the way to make crops grow.

attacks.name: Hypnosis
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: Implements shaped like it were discovered in ancient tombs. It is unknown if they are related.

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.effect: You may have your opponent switch their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It lived in the same environments as Rampardos. Their fossils have been found together—seemingly from after they'd fought to the finish.

attacks.name: Take Down
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 10 damage to itself.

flavorText: This Pokémon lived in primeval jungles. Few enemies would have been willing to square off against its heavily armored face, so it's thought.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: As it zooms through the sky, this Pokémon seems to be receiving signals of unknown origin, while transmitting signals of unknown purpose.

attacks.name: Zap Cannon
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't use Zap Cannon during your next turn.

flavorText: It has about three times the electrical power of Magnemite. For some reason, outbreaks of this Pokémon happen when lots of sunspots appear.
It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it's eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

attacks.name: Searching Magnet
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Metal Energy cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It sends out electromagnetic waves, which let it float through the air. Touching it while it's eating electricity will give you a full-body shock.

attacks.name: Gold Rush
attacks.effect: Discard any number of Metal Energy cards from your hand. This attack does 30 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

flavorText: These Pokémon are cherished in the Alola region, where they are thought to be feminine deities of the land incarnate.

attacks.name: Iron Head
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its golden hairs function as sensors. It pokes them out of its burrow to monitor its surroundings.

attacks.name: Abyssal Sleep
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep. Your opponent flips 2 coins instead of 1 between turns. If either of them is tails, that Pokémon is still Asleep.

rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
flavorText: It can lull people to sleep and make them dream. It is active during nights of the new moon.

attacks.name: Sticky Smokescreen
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips 2 coins. If either of them is tails, that attack does nothing.

flavorText: It sprays a stinky fluid from its tail. The fluid smells worse the longer it is allowed to fester.

attacks.name: Gas Bond
attacks.effect: Both Active Pokémon are now Confused.

flavorText: It protects itself by spraying a noxious fluid from its rear. The stench lingers for 24 hours.

attacks.name: Icy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

attacks.name: Evil Admonition
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each of your opponent's Pokémon that has an Ability.

flavorText: They dwell in cold places. This Pokémon's main food source in Alola is Vulpix and Sandshrew, which they carefully divide among their group.

attacks.name: Sneaky Smash
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you go second, and only on your first turn. Discard an Energy from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Ambush
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 more damage.

flavorText: It uses its claws to poke holes in eggs so it can slurp out the insides. Breeders consider it a scourge and will drive it away or eradicate it.

attacks.name: Rip and Run
attacks.effect: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand. Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: If its Murkrow cronies fail to catch food for it, or if it feels they have betrayed it, it will hunt them down wherever they are and punish them.

attacks.name: Mean Look
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: Seen as a symbol of bad luck, it's generally disliked. Yet it gives presents—objects that sparkle or shine—to Trainers it's close to.

attacks.name: Rock Hurl
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: They battle with hard berries for weapons. Their techniques are passed from the boss to the group, generation upon generation.

attacks.name: Sand Tomb
attacks.effect: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Dust Cannon
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 more damage for each Colorless in your opponent's Active Pokémon's Retreat Cost.

flavorText: It blasts internally stored sand from ports on its body to create a towering twister for attack.

attacks.name: Eleventh Hour Tackle
attacks.effect: If there are 3 or fewer cards in your deck, this attack does 130 more damage.

flavorText: It enshrouds itself with sand to protect itself from germs. It does not enjoy getting wet.

attacks.name: Missile Jab
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

flavorText: They can detect the species of a living being—and its emotions—from over half a mile away. They control auras and hunt their prey in packs.

attacks.name: Detect
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It's tough enough to run right through the night, and it's also a hard worker, but it's still just a youngster.

attacks.name: Clean Hit
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is an Evolution Pokémon, this attack does 60 more damage.

attacks.name: Wild Crash
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it is Knocked Out.

flavorText: Records exist of a revived fossil that evolved into Rampardos. It proceeded to escape and then destroy a skyscraper with a headbutt.
In rock layers where Cranidos fossils are found, the fossilized trunks of trees snapped in two are also often found.

attacks.name: Dark Flash
attacks.effect: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance.

attacks.name: Moon's Eclipse-GX
attacks.effect: You can use this attack only if you have more Prize cards remaining than your opponent. Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Full Moon Star
attacks.effect: For each of your opponent's Pokémon in play, attach a Psychic Energy card from your discard pile to your Pokémon in any way you like.

attacks.name: Psystorm
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage times the amount of Energy attached to all Pokémon.

rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
flavorText: Said to live in another world, this Pokémon devours light, drawing the moonless dark veil of night over the brightness of day.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: There's something accumulating around the black core within its hard shell. People think this Pokémon may come from another world.

attacks.name: Teleport
attacks.effect: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is gaseous and frail. It slowly grows as it collects dust from the atmosphere.

attacks.name: Lunar Payback
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, switch all damage counters on this Pokémon with those on your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Psychic
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Those who sleep holding Cresselia's feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

attacks.name: Crisis Dive
attacks.effect: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.

rules: ◇ (Prism Star) Rule: You can't have more than 1 ◇ card with the same name in your deck. If a ◇ card would go to the discard pile, put it in the Lost Zone instead.
flavorText: It was banished for its violence. It silently gazed upon the old world from the Distortion World.

attacks.name: Poison Jab
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Exact Revenge
attacks.effect: If any of your Fighting Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an opponent's attack during their last turn, this attack does 70 more damage.

flavorText: Its knuckle claws secrete a toxin so vile that even a scratch could prove fatal.

attacks.name: Swagger
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Inflating its poison sacs, it fills the area with an odd sound and hits flinching opponents with a poison jab.

attacks.name: Dangerous Stinger
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed and Poisoned.

flavorText: It has the power in its clawed arms to make scrap of a car. The tips of its claws release poison.

attacks.name: Hone Claws
attacks.effect: During your next turn, this Pokémon's Pierce attack's base damage is 90.

flavorText: It burrows under the sand to lie in wait for prey. Its tail claws can inject its prey with a savage poison.

attacks.name: Lightless World
attacks.effect: Put 2 Supporter cards from your discard pile into your hand.

attacks.name: Terrify
attacks.effect: If the Defending Pokémon is a Basic Pokémon, it can't attack during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.

attacks.name: Damage Transport
attacks.effect: Move 4 damage counters from each of your Pokémon to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Wind Wheel
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: Even while under careful observation, large flocks of Drifblim flying at dusk will inexplicably disappear from view.

attacks.name: Creepy Wind
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: If for some reason its body bursts, its soul spills out with a screaming sound.

attacks.name: Plasma Slice
attacks.effect: This Pokémon can't attack during your next turn.

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

attacks.name: Snuggly Generator
attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Pokémon that has the Nuzzle attack, search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Nuzzle
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: A pair may be seen rubbing their cheek pouches together in an effort to share stored electricity.

attacks.name: Volt Bolt
attacks.effect: Discard all Lightning Energy from this Pokémon. This attack does 150 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Luxray's ability to see through objects comes in handy when it's scouting for danger.

attacks.name: Disconnect
attacks.effect: Your opponent can't play any Item cards from their hand during their next turn.

flavorText: Strong electricity courses through the tips of its sharp claws. A light scratch causes fainting in foes.

attacks.name: Charge
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Lightning Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.
All of its fur dazzles if danger is sensed. It flees while the foe is momentarily blinded.

attacks.name: Steel Short
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Metal Pokémon, it is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: When it gets excited, it thumps its chest. With every thud, thunder roars and electric sparks shower all around.

attacks.name: Thunder
attacks.effect: This Pokémon does 30 damage to itself.

flavorText: Electricity leaks from it in amounts far greater than the amount of electricity it eats.

attacks.name: Deep Currents
attacks.effect: Shuffle 5 Water Energy cards from your discard pile into your deck.

attacks.name: Water Pulse
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It starts its life with a wondrous power that permits it to bond with any kind of Pokémon.

attacks.name: Frost Crush
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 more damage times the amount of Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

attacks.name: Wash Arrow
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

attacks.name: Frost Bullet
attacks.effect: This attack does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

attacks.name: Polar Spear-GX
attacks.effect: This attack does 50 damage for each damage counter on your opponent's Active Pokémon. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Hypno Hammer
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: It lives a quiet life on mountains that are perpetually covered in snow. It hides itself by whipping up blizzards.

attacks.name: Ice Shard
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Fighting Pokémon, this attack does 40 more damage.

flavorText: In the spring, it grows berries with the texture of frozen treats around its belly.

attacks.name: Agility
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

attacks.name: Aqua Blast
attacks.effect: Discard a Water Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: Its flotation sac developed as a result of pursuing aquatic prey. It can double as a rubber raft.
It inflates the flotation sac around its neck and pokes its head out of the water to see what is going on.

attacks.name: Total Command
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

attacks.name: Whirlpool
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: The three horns that extend from its beak attest to its power. The leader has the biggest horns.

attacks.name: Bubble Beam
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It lives a solitary life. Its wings deliver wicked blows that can snap even the thickest of trees.
Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

attacks.name: Splatter
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Because it is very proud, it hates accepting food from people. Its thick down guards it from cold.

attacks.name: Roar
attacks.effect: Your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.

flavorText: It exhales air colder than -58 degrees Fahrenheit. Elderly people in Alola call this Pokémon by an older name—Keokeo.

attacks.name: Spike Armor
attacks.effect: During your opponent's next turn, if this Pokémon is damaged by an attack (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.

flavorText: This Pokémon's steel spikes are sheathed in ice. Stabs from these spikes cause deep wounds and severe frostbite as well.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: An ancient tradition of Alolan festivals, still carried on to this day, is a competition to slide Sandshrew across ice as far as one can.

attacks.name: Searing Flame
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It gushes fire and poisonous gases from its nostrils. Its dung is an explosive substance and can be put to various uses.

attacks.name: Panic Poison
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned, Confused, and Poisoned.

attacks.name: Hunter's Nails
attacks.effect: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is affected by a Special Condition, this attack does 60 more damage.

flavorText: Filled with pheromones, its poisonous gas can be diluted to use in the production of luscious perfumes.
Volcanoes or dry, craggy places are its home. It emanates a sweet-smelling poisonous gas that attracts bug Pokémon, then attacks them.
Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

attacks.name: Burst Punch
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It tosses its enemies around with agility. It uses all its limbs to fight in its own unique style.

attacks.name: Super Singe
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Burned.

flavorText: It uses ceilings and walls to launch aerial attacks. Its fiery tail is but one weapon.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage for each heads.

flavorText: The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.
The gas made in its belly burns from its rear end. The fire burns weakly when it feels sick.

attacks.name: Fire Blaster
attacks.effect: You may discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 80 more damage.

flavorText: From its arm, it launches fireballs hotter than 3,500 degrees Fahrenheit. Its arm starts to melt when it fires a whole barrage.

attacks.name: Controlled Burn
attacks.effect: Discard the top card of your opponent's deck.

attacks.name: Flamethrower
attacks.effect: Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.

flavorText: When angered, it spouts brilliant fire from all over its body. It doesn't calm down until its opponent has burned to ash.
It delivers headbutts with the water bubble on its head. Small Pokémon get sucked into the bubble, where they drown.

attacks.name: Bubble
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

flavorText: It crawls onto the land in search of food. Its water bubble allows it to breathe and protects its soft head.

attacks.name: Coax
attacks.effect: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Pokémon of different types and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Soothing Scent
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep.

flavorText: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

attacks.name: Special Mow
attacks.effect: Discard a Special Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

flavorText: Its body is composed of plasma. It is known to infiltrate electronic devices and wreak havoc.

attacks.name: Grand Bloom-GX
attacks.effect: For each of your Benched Basic Pokémon, search your deck for a card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck. (You can't use more than 1 GX attack in a game.)

rules: Pokémon-GX rule: When your Pokémon-GX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Chomp Chomp
attacks.effect: Heal 20 damage from this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Loom Over
attacks.effect: This attack does 10 less damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon.

flavorText: It binds itself to trees in marshes. It attracts prey with its sweet-smelling drool and gulps them down.
If it senses strong sunlight, it opens its folded petals to absorb the sun's rays with its whole body.

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

flavorText: It evolves by sucking the energy out of the small ball where it had been storing nutrients.

attacks.name: Giga Drain
attacks.effect: Heal from this Pokémon the same amount of damage you did to your opponent's Active Pokémon.

attacks.name: Earthquake
attacks.effect: This attack does 20 damage to each of your Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

flavorText: Small Pokémon occasionally gather on its unmoving back to begin building their nests.

attacks.name: Mega Drain
attacks.effect: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.

flavorText: It knows where pure water wells up. It carries fellow Pokémon there on its back.
It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

attacks.name: Synthesis
attacks.effect: Search your deck for a Grass Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

flavorText: It undertakes photosynthesis with its body, making oxygen. The leaf on its head wilts if it is thirsty.

attacks.name: Inviting Poison
attacks.effect: Switch 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon with their Active Pokémon. The new Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.

attacks.name: Flower Tornado
attacks.effect: Move any number of Grass Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.

flavorText: With the movements of a dancer, it strikes with whips that are densely lined with poison thorns.

attacks.name: Petal Dance
attacks.effect: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads. This Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: Roselia that drink nutritionally rich springwater are said to reveal rare coloration when they bloom.

attacks.name: Supersonic
attacks.effect: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

flavorText: This six-legged Pokémon is easily capable of transporting an adult in flight. The wings on its tail help it stay balanced.

attacks.name: Super Speed
attacks.effect: Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to this Pokémon during your opponent's next turn.

flavorText: If it flaps its wings really fast, it can generate shock waves that will shatter windows in the area.

attacks.name: Continuous Eggsplosion
attacks.effect: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

flavorText: Its six eggs use telepathy to communicate among themselves. It is believed to carry plant genes and the genes of other species.
